title: A Rich Man Who Lamented | HG Amitasana Dasa
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: There was one gentleman who helped us in the initial days of Akshaya Patra. He was
very influential man. There is a committee at the national level which is
chaired by the finance minister and he was part of that committee and many
financial policies are reviewed more so with respect to taxation in that
committee and since he was very very influential and was very impressed by
the Akshaya Patra thing, he would invariably speak to some industrialists,
influential people and he helped us a lot. Since he was in an influential
position he could call anybody, any industrialist, he could even call let's
say Mr. Mukesh Ambani because financial policies which the government makes
impacts all businesses so they will have some relations. So he introduced some
industrialist to us in the initial days and somehow you know we got busy and he
got busy and some of the connection for some time got lost and then one day he
calls a devotee and says Swamiji when you come to Delhi please try to visit my
place and the voice was very very
very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very
gloomy, sad. So the devotees tried to inquire how are you? Long time haven't
met surely we'll definitely come to your place when we visit Delhi and it so
happened that next few days when the devotees were visiting Delhi they
visited his place.
Mind you were talking about not an ordinary person sometimes you know when
ordinary person faces a lot of difficulties,
faces some challenges in life. He's not able to find solution because he doesn't
have money, cannot afford treatment, already is in a financial mess. Here we
have an example of a person who is very influential among the you know top brass
in the country, makes national level additions. So the devotees visited his
house, the door was open, some servant came,
and they invited the devotees inside and this person then comes in limping very
very slowly and then he sits in the drawing room on the sofa and some of the
devotees who visited him could not you know make out just few years back he was
so much charged up so muchísimo thank you much la gente ha estado
full of entusiasm and here is completely here es mucho entusiasmo at the moment
completely be failed old.
sAPPARTE
So then course of the discussion, he says swamiji holds the hand and says
Swami ji, You are all very close to Krishna
You all pray so much! When you pray please pray that
i die very soon so it was like never expected these kind of words from a person
she said what happened why are you saying so this is swamiji what to say my last few
years my life has become a hell he's living in a big palatial house in delhi
servants are there everything is there all amenities are there
so my life has become a hell so he says somehow i have this severe backache
tried all kinds of treatments continuously it pains can't sleep can't sit any time of the day
it pains and now i am living for last few years on painkillers and now a time has come
even the painkillers have become ineffective because the body gets used to that's why many times
the doctors prescribe a higher dose and because of these painkillers which i've been taking every day
eight ten twelve tablets all the organs are now because of side effects after all it's chemical
it's not the body can't take it for a short while so he says because of that i have gastric problems
all kinds of digestion problems so life has become hell swamiji i wonder what's the use of living
like this so these are the examples of how sometimes we get a feeling that yes if somebody
is rich he can he's that way blessed he can afford treatment he can get admitted in the
best of hospitals he can have you know
but conditional life
doesn't spare anybody
prakriti sthani karshati it's an illusion which makes us no doubt money has a place
money can afford you it just like they say money can buy medicines but cannot buy health
some sayings are there money can buy house but not home so there's a difference between the two
lot of people living in palatial houses but there is no homeliness
there is no cordial relationship
the family members fights are going on so this is the meaning of conditional life which
irrespective of how much bank balance you have what status you have in the society
